title: Shopify & Monday Integration
excerpt:
In 2022 we brought a second Shopify store board into Monday. The first Shopify store was setup with Monday’s built-in Shopify integration (2020).

posts.author: a07 (Melissa M)
posts.cookedHtml:
In 2022 we brought a second Shopify store board into Monday. The first Shopify store was setup with Monday’s built-in Shopify integration (2020).

When we went to add the second store this year, we realized Monday had removed the integration due to Shopify’s changes on the back end.

We are really hoping Monday will bring back the built-in integration they originally had - Shopify is a widely used ecomm platform and I am sure many Monday users are annoyed they can’t easily pull in Shopify orders with the order URL. Such a simple feature but man do we miss it.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
I couldn’t agree more @a07, this is a function we miss as well. Since it’s departure we have gone to integrating Shopify and monday via make.com. Not as simplistic as the original integration but does offer a lot of control.

Thanks!
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: a07 (Melissa M)
posts.cookedHtml: Hmm we have been using Zapier - does make.com send through the order URL? Zapier does not.

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @a07 - I dont think so, but you can just create it on the fly as the orderID is sent along:

https://[shop_name].myshopify.com/admin/orders/[orderID] then store it in a link or text column in monday.

Mark

posts.author: Keem (Hakeem Baba)
posts.cookedHtml: I built a first version of a Shopify Integration on the marketplace . I will be happy to add more integration recipes if there is a some considerable interest in this integration.

posts.author: LillyBates (Lilly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Your experience with integrating Shopify and Monday showcases how software changes can impact workflows. It’s frustrating when a feature that was once available becomes unavailable due to external changes.
The convenience of easily pulling in Shopify orders with order URLs can indeed streamline processes for many users. I understand how that simple yet effective feature could be missed.

If you were exploring alternatives or solutions for this integration challenge, platforms like https://transformagency.com/magento-custom-development-services/ might provide insights. While the link refers to Magento custom development services, exploring similar services might uncover solutions for integrating Shopify with Monday.

posts.author: carlosharyy (Carlos Hariis)
posts.cookedHtml: Integrating Shopify with Monday.com streamlines ecommerce operations by syncing orders, inventory, and customer data directly into Monday boards, enabling teams to manage fulfillment, customer service, and marketing campaigns efficiently. Using tools like Zapier, Make (Integromat), or custom API integrations, businesses can automate workflows such as creating tasks for new orders, triggering inventory alerts, and tracking campaign performance—all without coding. This integration centralizes data, enhances team collaboration, improves visibility across departments, and ensures real-time updates, making it a powerful solution for scaling ecommerce operations professionally and efficiently.
